allowed_claims: BHSM includes executable theorem-closure attempt machinery.
The sprint attempts X_ch, neutrino basis/scale, and standalone CP O_int closure using local artifacts and proof gates.
A theorem is promoted only if formal statement, callable, artifact, provenance, and tests support promotion.
Open theorem blockers remain open when required objects are missing.
author_axiom_policy: Author-supplied axioms remain conditional and cannot be relabeled action-derived.
closure_policy: CLOSED_DERIVED_ACTION_LEVEL requires every relevant proof gate to pass.
forbidden_claims: The theorem closure sprint empirically validates BHSM.
X_ch is production-ready without an explicit action-backed theorem.
The neutral kernel is a physical neutrino mass matrix without basis/scale/Dirac-Majorana theorem.
CP holonomy is a standalone production interaction without O_int attachment theorem.
Runtime software gates are validated without live external validation.
FeynRules/UFO/MadGraph readiness is achieved without runtime validation.
partial_localization_policy: A partial localization is not a production prediction.
reference_value_policy: Reference and PDG values are comparison-only and never theorem inputs.
runtime_gate_policy: Runtime-disabled gates remain disabled until live external validation passes.
